channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Build a Voice Appointment Reminder Bot: AI Calls That Confirm, Reschedule, or Cancel
item.description: No-shows are expensive. Whether you're running a clinic, a salon, a law office, or a SaaS demo pipeline — every missed appointment is lost revenue. The traditional fix is a human calling each...

item.title: How I Built a Static Analyzer for Python Env Variables in ~700 Lines
item.description: I keep forgetting to add new env vars to .env.production before deploying. A few times it was harmless, a few times it was not. At some point it was easier to write a tool than to fix myself. The...

item.title: How to Run LLMs Locally When Cloud AI Gets Too Invasive
item.description: If you've been paying attention to the AI space lately, you've probably noticed a trend: cloud AI providers are tightening the screws on identity verification. We're talking government IDs, facial...

item.title: 🌍 Deep-Time Mirror: An AI Lens into Our Ecological Soul For Earth Day Edition.
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Earth Day Edition (April 22) What I Built I built the Deep-Time Mirror, an immersive, AI-powered "temporal lens" that allows users to witness the...

item.title: Programs for Pincode, Gmail, and Mobile Number Validation
item.description: 1.Write a program to check whether a given Gmail ID is valid or not. Python def verify_Gmail(Gmail): i=0 while i<len(Gmail): current=Gmail[i] i+=1 if current>="a" and current<="z": continue; elif...

item.title: We audited every claim in our repos and found 14 files with wrong numbers
item.description: Last week a bot embarrassed us. Cursor Bugbot ran across five PRs on our agent security testing framework and filed nine real issues: an HTTP 413 handler that returned an empty body, undefined...

item.title: I Built a Code Archaeology Engine for AI — Here's Why Claude and Cursor Keep Forgetting Your Architecture
item.description: TL;DR: AI coding assistants have zero architectural memory. Every session starts from scratch. I built LORE — an open-source MCP server with 13 analyzers that gives your AI deep understanding of...

item.title: Introduction to React: A Beginner’s Guide
item.description: Introduction React is a popular JavaScript library used for building user interfaces, especially for single-page applications (SPAs). It was developed and maintained by Meta (formerly Facebook)...

item.title: Kiwi-chan Progress Report: Steady Mining!
item.description: Kiwi-chan's Latest Escapades: From Logs to Looming Boredom (and Back to Logs!) Hey there, fellow tech explorers and Minecraft fanatics! It's devlog time for our favorite autonomous AI, Kiwi-chan....

item.title: Open WebUI vs. ChatGPT: Which One Is Right for You in 2026?

item.title: Tokenmaxxing, OpenAI’s shopping spree, and the AI Anxiety Gap
item.description: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps...

item.title: The best budget smartphones you can buy
item.description: Some of us take a kind of “eat to live” rather than a “live to eat” approach to gadgets. They’re tools that help you get things done, not something you want to invest a lot of time or money in. If...

item.title: Dairy Queen is putting an AI chatbot in its drive-thrus
item.description: Dairy Queen is becoming the latest fast food chain to get in on AI, as it's bringing a chatbot to dozens of its drive-thrus across the US and Canada. It aims to help speed up drive-thru service...

item.title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body
item.description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the tradeoff may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.

item.title: The AirPods Pro 3 are $50 off right now, nearly matching their best-ever price

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals
item.description: The company says Claude Design is intended to help people like founders and product managers without a design background share their ideas more easily.
